primary_problem: The clinic needs a system that speeds up lab input and lab output during daily operations.
core_product_direction: Schema-driven laboratory platform with an exam/form builder.

must_not_reopen_without_evidence: LAN-on-by-default desktop setting for no-hassle same-network access
human_or_client_input_still_needed: manual QA of the installed app
user review of the root UI/UX redesign direction and the proposed Safety IA
clean-PC restore drill expectations and external backup folder decision

implemented: PyInstaller plus Inno Setup packaging foundation
persistent installed data under %ProgramData%\NDHI\LabRecords
replaceable app binaries under %ProgramFiles%\NDHI\LabRecords
browser preference setting with auto, Edge, Chrome, and default-browser modes
LAN mode defaults on for fresh installs and binds to 0.0.0.0:8114
host PC still opens through 127.0.0.1:8114
installer-created Windows Firewall rule limited to Private/Domain local subnet
Settings -> Desktop app shows hostname/IP links, readiness cards, copy actions, full QR view, and downloadable SVG QR
QR is generated locally with segno and prefers IP fallback when available

Settings -> Desktop app shows local/external backup status, automatic daily backup status, external backup folder, retention count, manual backup, latest-backup verification actions, and admin-only restore with RESTORE confirmation
daily automatic backup runs while the app is open, creates a verified daily-auto archive when no verified backup exists for the current day, and records scheduler status under runtime config

restore verifies the selected archive, creates an emergency pre-restore backup, restores database and uploads, preserves machine-local desktop config by default, clears the current session, and sends the admin back to login using an account from the restored backup
source Inno installer script attempts a verified pre-update backup before replacing binaries when an existing runtime database is present
packaging scripts include the NDHI icon for the packaged executable and Inno Setup wrapper
not_release_complete: real LAN scan/connect QA on clinic-like network
clean-PC restore drill and Windows validation of scheduled/external/pre-update backup behavior

live_theme_foundation.current_toggle: integrated shell light/dark toggle in the top-right global header with local persistence and system fallback; public auth screens use the same inline topbar placement and no floating fallback remains

notes: This is the first records-first live reskin pass under the Clinical Depth Luxe direction.

A direct follow-up pass strengthened light-mode heading contrast, added real select styling, and added actual field-shell structure so records no longer read like the old scaffold with only recolored surfaces.
The next follow-up pass focused on dark-mode fidelity first so records move closer to the locked Clinical Depth Luxe Darker sample before more light-mode polish resumes.

Another dark-mode follow-up also aligned the records typography and button treatment more closely with the locked darker sample so the darker experience is not relying on the old scaffold-era font and control feel.

A live browser-audited records follow-up then pushed actual structure too: /records now groups its top-shell actions and search more intentionally, and the record-start flow now lives more directly inside the records hub instead of as a full separate ceremony page.

A broader live pass also pushed library.css, auth.css, and new-form.css into the same luxe family, so forms library, auth, clinic settings, user settings, and start-new-form now read less like the older warm utility scaffolds.
Native file upload controls were also styled through theme.css so clinic/logo/image flows do not drop back to default browser chrome.

Direct browser-audited cleanup then fixed the remaining obvious dark-mode mismatches in record status chips, accent-label strength, settings/users stat cards, and the current builder content plus preview panels.
The current builder shell now sits inside the same non-print luxe family, but print is still intentionally excluded from this reskin.

A shared authenticated shell now sits under the non-print app too: shell.css, shell.js, and _authenticated_shell.html provide a thin global-header plus hidden-drawer model, with separate page-local headers across records, forms, settings, and the builder workspace variant.

The authenticated shell now keeps global navigation hidden in a left drawer opened from the top bar, while the actual page title and actions live in page-local headers so the global chrome stays light.
A follow-up consistency cleanup also normalized auth.css, library.css, new-form.css, and app.css against the shared theme tokens so the live app stops leaking older hardcoded warm-palette surfaces.
Live browser checks were rerun across login, records, forms, new-form, clinic settings, and builder to specifically catch light-mode visibility and cross-mode consistency issues.

The next structural correction then landed at the page-composition level: records, forms, and settings now move toward a content-first model with lighter page headers, compact toolbars, open sections, and cards reserved mostly for actual records, forms, and users instead of every wrapper block.

The shell architecture is no longer the primary UI question now; future non-print refinement should preserve the current shell and continue reducing visual weight through surface discipline instead of reopening navigation structure again.

A follow-up list-page pass then tightened the behavior rule too: list-heavy pages like records, forms, and users now move toward bounded scroll regions and lighter utility rows, while edit/detail pages keep normal page scroll.

A later architecture-improvement pass then made the current records flow more queue-first, reduced the edit/view top chrome into a compact summary shell, lowered action density in the forms library, and removed the separate builder feedback bar in favor of a lighter top app bar status treatment.

The latest compact work-surface pass flattened the builder stage chrome into one command bar, tightened records/forms/users list surfaces, added a searchable form picker for both the new-record modal and /records/new fallback, consolidated user management into one searchable/filterable directory, and hardened global [hidden] display handling in theme.css.

A Settings IA pass then made Settings visible to every signed-in user, moved password/security into Settings > My account, kept Clinic profile and Users & access admin-only, simplified the drawer footer to account identity plus Log out, and standardized drawer icons into one premium-style 24px stroke system.

The account/profile foundation then added profile photo upload/remove under Settings > My account, shell avatar display with initials fallback, read-only email/login identity so accountability stays anchored to stable account identifiers, staff avatar/initial identity inside Users & access, and a dedicated Manage user page for admin full-name/role correction plus password reset.

Settings > My account now uses the same compact utility-card system as the newer Settings account admin screens, so profile, password, and account identity no longer fall back to the older heavy auth-grid composition.

Settings > Clinic profile now uses that same compact utility-card system for clinic identity, contact details, logo upload, and brand preview, keeping the future print-branding base cleaner without starting the heavy print-template work yet.

The Settings utility follow-up then brought Settings > Create user and Manage user into the same compact utility-card treatment as the Forms utility screens, so manual account fallback and user maintenance no longer fall back to older heavy auth panels.

The public auth follow-up then moved /login, /request-account, and /setup to the same compact utility-card language too, replacing the older heavy auth panel plus long helper-list composition with one direct form card and one compact facts card.
Settings > Users & access now keeps search and status filters inside the Accounts directory header, so the staff list reads as one directory surface instead of a separate toolbar plus list.
/records/history now keeps search and status filters inside the History section header, so lookup reads as one history surface and search preserves the active Completed/Drafts/All filter.

Root UI/UX Phase 3A has now landed for the records workbench/history surfaces: /records and /records/history use lighter row-like record surfaces, compact history filter controls, and the record start modal plus /records/new fallback share a split searchable picker layout.

Root UI/UX Phase 4A has now landed for record entry/view: /records/{id}/edit uses a flatter field-first panel, compact summary readiness, a sticky edit-form action bar with Complete and print primary, and safe long-text wrapping; /records/{id} shows clearer draft/completed/voided state hierarchy without accidentally docking view-page void actions.

Root UI/UX Phase 5A has now landed for the shared modal foundation: authenticated non-print pages expose window.NAICApp.confirm(), record data-confirm forms use the shell modal instead of browser confirm(), and delete draft, void record, and dirty internal navigation are covered.

Root UI/UX Phase 5B has now landed for safety forms: shell.js owns a generic app-wide data-confirm submit handler, /settings/desktop backup/verify/settings/restore forms use the shared modal system, and restore backup now gets a destructive safe-cancel-first modal while keeping the typed RESTORE server guard.

Root UI/UX Phase 6A has now landed for Safety IA: admins see Safety as a top-level drawer item, /safety owns backup/restore/LAN/QR/browser-desktop operational controls, /settings/desktop remains as a compatibility route, and Safety actions post through /safety aliases so users stay in Safety after operations.

Root UI/UX Phase 7 has been recalibrated as builder polish/QA, not a rewrite: the current recursive Container/Field canvas, optional preview, workspace rail, and command bar stay; do not reintroduce a permanent heavy inspector or duplicate content outline.

Authenticated pages now include topbar and drawer View size controls: scaled virtual app viewport from 50% to 200% in 5% steps, reset to 100%, persisted in localStorage, and intentionally separate from print output.

The builder stabilization pass is now landed too: builder-specific light/dark surfaces are tokenized, organizer rows no longer inherit primary button styling, preview is opt-in instead of open by default, command actions are grouped, and the workspace header no longer sticks over builder content while scrolling.

The recursive builder integration has now passed a real-browser QA pass too: duplicate-form startup, nested containers and fields, choice options, numeric ranges, subtree copy/remove, mixed advanced-block ordering, save/reload persistence, light/dark contrast, and desktop/tablet/mobile layouts were checked directly.

Builder subtree copies now refresh block and option IDs recursively, SortableJS ordering now resolves real collection positions even while Advanced utility blocks are hidden, advanced utility cards expose drag handles, and tablet/mobile workspace tabs stay compact and reachable above the editor.
The preferred feel is premium, modern, calm, and authored rather than generic admin minimalism.
Dark mode should stay curated and atmospheric, not neon and not pure black.
The live app now has a shared theme foundation plus a browser-audited non-print reskin and consistency pass; these standalone files remain the locked visual north star for future UI work.

As of 2026-06-07, the active UI direction is no longer only bug/polish: the user requested a root UI/UX critique and accepted a workflow-first redesign plan. Continue from docs/handoff/UI_UX_ROOT_REDESIGN_PLAN.md before touching shell, scroll, density, records, modals, Safety, or builder UI.

The first root UI/UX Phase 1 pass has landed: authenticated screens now use a fixed-height app frame with body/document scroll disabled, app-shell-main as the normal scroll owner, compact global/page headers, tighter record-edit summary/readiness chrome, and Complete and print as the primary record-entry action. Visual QA screenshots and metrics live under output/ui-ux-phase1/.

The first root UI/UX Phase 2 atom pass has landed: shared theme.css control tokens now compact primary/ghost buttons, fields, chips, and modal radius; the broad button:not(...) primary selector was removed so password toggles, shell icon buttons, modal scrims, and record-picker cards keep their own treatment; public auth, shell, records, forms library, and settings CSS links were cache-busted to 20260607-ui-root-phase2. Visual QA screenshots and computed scroll/style metrics live under output/ui-ux-phase2/.

The first root UI/UX Phase 3A records workbench/history pass has landed: /records and /records/history now use lighter row-like record surfaces, compact history search/filter controls, and the new-record modal plus /records/new fallback share the split searchable picker layout; records CSS was cache-busted to 20260607-ui-root-phase3. Visual QA screenshots and computed scroll/style metrics live under output/ui-ux-phase3/.

The first root UI/UX Phase 4A record entry/view pass has landed: /records/{id}/edit now has a compact readiness summary, flatter field-first entry panel, scoped sticky bottom action dock, Complete and print as the primary draft completion action, and safe long-text wrapping; /records/{id} now has clearer draft/completed/voided state hierarchy. Records CSS was cache-busted to 20260607-ui-root-phase4. Visual QA screenshots and computed scroll/style metrics live under output/ui-ux-phase4/.

The first root UI/UX Phase 5A shared modal foundation has landed: _authenticated_shell.html, shell.css, and shell.js provide a reusable decision modal through window.NAICApp.confirm(); records.js routes data-confirm forms and dirty internal navigation through it; Delete draft and Void record now have structured safe-cancel-first destructive dialogs. Shell CSS/JS and records JS were cache-busted to 20260607-ui-root-phase5a*. Visual QA screenshots and modal/focus/overflow metrics live under output/ui-ux-phase5a/.

Root UI/UX Phase 5B has landed: shell.js now owns a generic app-wide [data-confirm] submit handler, /settings/desktop safety forms declare explicit modal copy for backup creation, local/external verification, desktop settings save, and restore backup; shell JS was cache-busted to 20260607-ui-root-phase5b. Visual QA screenshots and modal/focus/overflow metrics live under output/ui-ux-phase5b/.

Root UI/UX Phase 6A has landed: the visible admin IA now includes a top-level Safety page at /safety for backup, restore, LAN/app access health, QR, browser preference, and desktop runtime controls; /settings/desktop remains available as a compatibility path but is no longer advertised in the Settings subnav. Visual QA screenshots and route/overflow metrics live under output/ui-ux-phase6a/.

Root UI/UX Phase 7 is now locked as a no-rewrite builder polish/QA pass: keep the current recursive Content model, optional preview, workspace rail, and command bar; avoid returning to a permanent inspector or duplicate outline model.
The authenticated shell now has app-level View size controls in the topbar and drawer, using a scaled virtual app viewport rather than browser zoom; keep print pages excluded from this scaling.
Quick light-mode audits were also run across the main non-print screens so the next review should not immediately hit obvious contrast regressions.
Builder should stay denser than records, forms, and settings; the calmer content-first composition rule is mainly for the everyday non-builder product surfaces.
Directness is now part of the live UI rule too: obvious screens should not over-explain themselves, and helper text should remain only where it prevents mistakes or clarifies non-obvious actions.

The previous non-print reskin remains useful context, but the current accepted product direction is a compact clinic operations workbench with one scroll owner per screen, stronger light-mode contrast, denser records workflows, a real modal system, Complete and print as the primary record-entry completion action, and a likely top-level admin Safety area for backup/restore/LAN health.

alignment_lock: keep the engine flexible and future-proof
keep the visible UX calm and simple for non-technical users
keep the container and form tree direction
keep the ordered block direction under the hood
keep one top-level Content editor; do not reintroduce a sibling Arrange or Layout pane
do not reintroduce presets into the active product path right now
do not add new product surfaces unless they clearly reduce complexity or complete the current core goal
continue simplifying the current builder instead of restarting the architecture debate

records_runtime_foundation: /records now exists as the Work view for the small-clinic daily draft queue
/records/history now exists as the History view for completed lookup and search
/records/history?status=draft now exists as the lookup path for older drafts outside the recent Work queue
the normal new-record start path is now modal-first from the records views instead of staying as a separate ceremony page
choosing a form from that records picker creates the draft immediately and redirects straight to /records/{id}/edit
/records/new now stays only as the fallback deep-link picker page

/records now behaves more directly like a work queue too: the old metric strip is gone, Work and History expose clearer local-nav counts, and completed lookup plus search now live in History instead of the default work screen
when the Work queue truncates recent drafts, it now exposes a direct View all drafts action into the History Drafts filter
/records/history now keeps search and status filters inside the History section header, and search preserves the active Completed/Drafts/All filter
/records/history now paginates 40 records per page and record links preserve the current History search/filter/page when returning from view, edit, or print
/records/{id}/edit now supports the first calm server-rendered record-entry flow
/records/{id} now shows a read-only record view
records now surface quiet accountability metadata too: created by, last updated by, and the latest activity timestamp are visible in the live records flow

record completion is calmer but stricter now too: drafts still stay flexible, but Complete now enforces configured record identity fields plus any builder-marked required field answers from the form design
record entry now also offers Complete and print, using the same completion validation before redirecting directly to /records/{id}/print

normal record Print is completed-only now: draft edit/view no longer expose a generic Print action, direct draft /records/{id}/print redirects back to edit with a clear warning, and Complete and print remains the validated print path while editing a draft

record-entry polish is calmer now too: ordinary draft saves return with a quiet Saved the draft banner, Sex now uses the same standard select in create and edit, and the image file hint no longer shows the old broken separator artifact

record edit is more self-guiding now too: draft records already show a quiet readiness panel while editing, and that same panel flips between blocked and ready-to-complete states instead of waiting for a failed submit first

record edit now keeps Save draft, Complete, and Complete and print in a sticky bottom action dock scoped to the edit form, with Complete and print as the primary action and action-specific progress text

record edit and view now start faster too: the older heavy record hero/meta stack was replaced by a lighter compact summary shell, summary readiness no longer uses a large pre-field checklist, and deeper audit metadata now lives in a calmer collapsible Record info area instead of dominating the top of the page
record view now wraps long titles and recorded values safely, including extreme patient-name cases, so it does not create horizontal overflow inside the app shell
record edit is safer now too: it shows a quiet dirty-state label and browser leave protection now warns before accidental navigation away from unsaved changes
record edit now uses the shared app modal for internal dirty-navigation decisions, while browser-native beforeunload remains only for tab close/reload
record delete draft and void completed record decisions now use the shared shell decision modal instead of native browser confirm dialogs
record entry, record view, and print now render utility blocks more honestly too: note text, divider captions, and sample tables no longer fall back to generic placeholders

the record header is more future-proof too: new, edit, view, and print can use generic record identity configured from the form builder, with old patient/case wording treated as compatibility fallback
records are now stored separately from forms and point to a frozen form_version_id
the first records API surface now exists at /api/records/bootstrap, /api/records, /api/records/{id}, and /api/records/{id}/complete
record status lifecycle now includes draft, completed, deleted for soft-deleted drafts, and voided for completed records that are invalidated with an audit reason
image answer fields now support a first real upload, replace, preview, serve, and remove flow during record entry
/records/{id}/print now exists as the first browser-printable record/result surface and now reads form-version print_config for accent color, density, visibility toggles, and summary rows
auth_settings_foundation: /setup now exists as the first-run admin setup screen
/login now exists as the main sign-in screen
/request-account now exists for staff account requests
/settings/account now exists as the personal account settings screen
/settings/clinic now exists as the clinic profile and branding settings screen
/change-password now exists for first-login and self-service password changes
/settings/users now exists as the first admin user-management screen
the auth model is now hybrid: request account plus admin approval plus admin manual-create fallback
login now accepts email or login ID
admin-created accounts are forced to change password on first login

auth feedback is steadier now too: pending-account login explains that approval is still needed, disabled-account login explains that admin access is needed again, and successful password changes now redirect back to Records with a quiet success banner
/login, /request-account, and /setup now use compact utility-card layouts with one direct form card and one compact facts card instead of the older heavy auth-panel/helper-list composition
visible role gating is now active for Admin and Medtech
clinic profile data and clinic logo upload/remove now exist as the branding base for current and future print work
Settings > Clinic profile now uses compact utility-card layouts for clinic details, logo upload, and brand preview instead of the older profile-grid/profile-card composition
signed-in users can now upload/remove their own profile photo from Settings > My account, and the shell uses it in the account button and drawer identity with initials fallback
Settings > My account now uses compact utility-card layouts for profile, password, and read-only account identity instead of the older heavy auth-grid composition
Settings > Users & access now displays uploaded staff avatars when available, with initials fallback on account cards
Settings > Users & access now keeps search and status filters in the Accounts directory header instead of a separate toolbar block
admins can now open /settings/users/{id}/edit from the directory to correct full name/role and reset another user's temporary password
Settings > Create user and Manage user now share compact utility-card layouts with clearer identity/access sections instead of older heavy auth panels
email and login ID remain read-only in the personal account screen so identity changes do not undermine accountability
authenticated_shell_foundation: a shared authenticated shell now exists through app/naic_builder/static/shell.css, app/naic_builder/static/shell.js, and app/naic_builder/templates/_authenticated_shell.html
authenticated records, forms, settings, and builder pages now sit inside one role-aware product shell
the shell now provides a thin global header, a hidden left navigation drawer on demand, and separate page-local headers for page-level actions and back patterns
the nav treatment is intentionally lighter too: helper-copy under nav items is gone, nav uses icons plus short labels, and the old Compact/Expand text control is gone
the builder now uses the same product family through a workspace shell variant instead of living outside the app shell
real login-path smoke already passed for the new shell across records, forms, settings, and builder routes
engine_foundation: a persisted generic container/form library tree foundation now exists in the backend
the compatibility tree is exposed at /api/library/tree
current forms are backfilled into that tree automatically
the builder still uses the older fields plus sections schema shape under the hood
form reads now expose block_schema as the active form shape
api/forms/{slug} now carries the ordered-block view directly via block_schema
live create and update flows now use the block-based form_schema contract only
each form version now stores a real block_schema_json payload alongside the legacy schema_json
startup now backfills missing stored block schemas for older versions
non-legacy block kinds like note, divider, and table can now be preserved in stored block schema even when the compatibility projection skips them

the frontend builder draft is now block-first too: the live editor state no longer keeps a duplicated draft.schema, and setup details like Key and Notes now read and write directly through block_schema.meta
builder saves now go out through block_schema instead of posting the legacy fields plus sections shape directly
the current focused editor and live preview now also read and write through block-backed paths for real edit flows
top-level content editing now operates through block-backed collection handling while the visible UI stays calm
advanced mode now keeps one real Content pane for the ordered block tree instead of exposing a sibling Layout pane
the live preview now follows actual root block order, including separated top-field clusters when top-level fields appear in multiple positions
advanced mode can now add real note, divider, and table blocks in the same Content flow without pushing them into the calmer default editing panes
the live preview can now render note, divider, and table blocks from stored block_schema while the default panes remain focused on ordinary fields and sections
selected sections can now add note, divider, and table blocks too, but only in advanced mode so the normal section flow stays calm by default
selected groups can now add note, divider, and table blocks in advanced mode too, and the live preview now renders those nested utility blocks from the real block tree
selected sections and groups now keep nested organizers and focused editors inside the same Content flow, so there is no separate child-layout workspace anymore
when a section contains advanced utility blocks, the default section editor hides them and shows a quiet hint instead of exposing extra controls in the standard flow
richer stored block schema now survives builder hydration correctly, so advanced-only blocks remain intact after normal save and reload flows
/forms/new now uses real container choices from the persisted library tree instead of the older one-level grouped-folder list
the first save path can now carry a real library_parent_node_key, so new drafts can keep their intended container parent without collapsing back to a one-level folder assumption
library_behavior: /forms now renders directly from the real persisted container and form tree instead of the older one-level grouped library view
the library can now show both root-level forms and folders in one calm tree-first browse surface
library search now works against full folder path text instead of only one-level group names
the forms library is lighter now too: primary actions stay visible, secondary actions now live behind smaller More menus, and the top-level jump control is no longer always expanded

the latest forms library pass makes /forms read more like a compact file browser: folder/form rows use object icons, overflow actions are icon-only with accessible labels, and search now shows live form/folder counts plus a clear action only while filtering
folder cards in /forms can now launch folder-scoped creation directly via new form here and new folder here
folder cards in /forms can now also launch edit folder, so the visible tree flow is no longer create-only
folder cards in /forms can now also launch move, and form cards can now launch move too, so the visible tree flow now supports relocation as well as browse, create, and edit
the library top bar now also has a direct new folder path for root-level folder creation
new form, duplicate, and open builder flows are wired from the library
guided_create_behavior: new form creation now goes through /forms/new
the guided create screen collects form name, folder, and start method before opening the builder
start methods now include blank and duplicate existing form
/forms/new can now intentionally place a form at the top level instead of forcing every new form into a folder
/forms/new duplicate choices now use the real tree too, showing full folder paths instead of the older grouped optgroup list
/forms/new can now also create a brand-new folder inside an existing folder, and the first save path resolves that pending nested folder into the real library tree
/forms/new is visually lighter now too: the old three-step panel stack is now one guided setup card plus a compact summary, while the same hidden builder handoff values stay intact
/folders/new now exists as a small standalone folder-creation screen, so empty folders can be created directly before any forms exist inside them
/folders/edit now gives folders a real management path too: they can be renamed, and they can be deleted once empty

/folders/move and /forms/move now exist as calm single-purpose move screens, so folders and forms can change parent location inside the real tree without dropping back to the old one-level group model
folder create/edit/move and form move now share a compact utility-card style, so the smaller admin screens stay consistent with the Forms library and guided create flow
preset support has been deliberately removed from the active product path so the builder can stay focused on the core flow
builder bootstrap no longer includes preset data
advanced layout no longer exposes insert preset or save preset actions
builder duplicate actions now route through the guided create screen instead of forking immediately in place
workspace_behavior: the current Content pane is a recursive in-place canvas: containers collapse and expand where they live, fields edit inside their cards, and deeper details open inline only when needed
subtree copy is identity-safe now: every copied block and copied choice option receives a fresh recursive ID
mixed visible/hidden ordering is safe now: SortableJS maps visible node paths back to real stored collection positions so hidden Advanced utility blocks do not corrupt reorder results
tablet and mobile workspace navigation now stays above the editor as compact tabs; it no longer becomes a tall vertical block or falls below a long form
the builder workspace now uses a left outline plus one focused editing context at a time
the default workspace now lands on a single Content pane driven by real root block order instead of splitting the main flow into separate ungrouped-fields and sections panes
the live top-level builder flow now stays on just Basics, Content, and Save
the root Content pane now inserts new top-level blocks relative to the current selected block when possible, so the main workspace follows real root order instead of old bucket placement rules
the left outline now follows that same root content model too, showing real top-level content items instead of a section-only shortcut list
in advanced mode, the root Content pane can now add note, divider, and table blocks directly without forcing users into another pane just to place them
the old top of form area is now reframed as free fields
default shared record details are tucked into advanced mode instead of staying in the primary form-details surface
the content pane now uses a compact root organizer plus one focused editor

nested section and group content now also inserts relative to the currently selected child block when possible, so deeper editing follows real ordered block behavior instead of always appending at the end
selected groups can now add nested groups in the normal flow too, so grouped content is less locked to a field-only structure
selected groups now use the same compact child organizer plus one focused child editor pattern as selected sections, instead of spilling every child card open at once
the old separate root ungrouped-fields and sections panes are no longer active workspace paths; the builder now uses one real root Content path instead of keeping both models alive
the old root section-only focus path has now been removed from the active builder flow, so the workspace no longer keeps that extra root shortcut model alive behind the scenes
root and nested Content add actions now insert relative to the current selected block when possible, so the true ordered-block surface no longer blindly appends new blocks
advanced and fallback wording is calmer now too: the builder now prefers content and item language over more technical block wording in the visible UI
the live preview now also uses calmer root labels like Top content instead of the older Top fields or Layout wording for mixed root content
repeated add-button clusters are now collapsed into a quieter Add menu in Content, sections, and groups, so deeper editing feels less like managing buckets and more like inserting content pieces
focused group cards are lighter too: the dead disabled Type = Group row is gone, and the focused spotlight now uses quieter summary copy like Nested content instead of repeating mechanical metadata

focused field cards are lighter too: their focused state no longer repeats the field-type label above the title, their spotlight only shows truly useful metadata, advanced labels like Normal and Unit are shorter, and multi-cluster preview labels now read More content instead of awkward numbering like Top content 2
focused section cards are lighter too: the duplicate Section spotlight strip is gone, and deeper editing now stays in the same focused Content flow instead of jumping to a separate layout action
focused field and option editors are flatter too: the extra focused spotlight panels are gone, so those editors now read more like direct content editing and less like schema inspector cards
preview root clusters now use calmer labels like Details and More details instead of Top content
focused section, group, and utility cards are flatter too: redundant kind chips and utility spotlights are gone, so those editors now spend less space repeating what the item already is

organizer rows and the left outline are quieter too: named sections and groups no longer repeat redundant Section or Group subtitles, while field and utility cues still appear when they actually help with scanning
field organizers are quieter too: plain text and number fields no longer repeat type subtitles, while higher-signal cues like Dropdown, Date, Time, and utility kinds stay visible for faster scanning
field input wording is calmer too: the picker now says Input, with simpler choices like Text, Choices, and Date & time

field reference handling is more print-ready too: the live builder now uses Reference in the active field UI, numeric fields now use a Normal range with From / To, and choice options can be marked Counts as normal for future abnormal highlighting

the builder can now model Image as a real answer type too: the active schema can save image fields cleanly, while the preview stays honest that actual file upload belongs to the future fill-up runtime
legacy compatibility is preserved too: builder storage now keeps reference_text as the active block prop while still projecting it back into legacy normal_value where older storage expects it

the visible field UX stays calm too: the duplicate top Reference heading is gone, a tiny hint stays above the inputs, Normal range gets one small line for meaning, and choice options stay simple without extra tooltip clutter
active organizer rows are quieter too: the old Editing pills are gone from the outline and nested organizers, so focus now reads through the active highlight instead of extra status chips
advanced labels and helper copy are calmer too: shorter labels like Key and Notes, plus tighter Basics/Content/Save help copy, now keep the builder less technical without adding extra clutter
the old shared Record defaults selector is now hidden from the visible builder flow, so setup stays closer to the flexible lego model while backend compatibility remains intact
the guided /forms/new flow is less legacy too: it no longer carries old grouping/order hidden fields into the builder, only the tree/location data the current flow actually needs
the frontend draft logic is less legacy too: new and duplicated drafts no longer depend on old grouping/order fields just to keep the current builder working
library-tree sync is stronger too: FormDefinition location metadata is now backfilled from real library nodes, so page flows rely less on old group_name/group_kind fallbacks
top-level drafts are more stable too: renaming a top-level form now keeps the fallback location state in sync, so the builder no longer shows a stale old location name
the builder no longer tracks common_field_set_id in the active frontend draft path, so the visible editing flow is less tied to the old shared-metadata model
serialized form responses no longer expose common_field_set_id either, so the active API shape is less tied to the old shared-metadata model

serialized form responses no longer expose group_name, group_kind, group_order, or form_order; the live API now exposes only tree-first location_* metadata, and the builder reads that location state directly

the visible builder location helpers now read from location_* directly too; the sync helper now just keeps location labels and node keys consistent instead of maintaining old grouped-era shadow metadata
the active save contract is leaner too: FormSavePayload no longer declares group_name, and legacy callers are mapped into location_name during validation instead of staying as a first-class field
FormDefinition.common_field_set_id is no longer actively mirrored during seed/create flows, and update flows now clear that old mirror field instead of keeping it in sync with the schema

the active schema and block normalizers are less special too: new create, update, and seed flows no longer inject a synthetic common_field_set_id into saved schema_json or block_schema_json, so that hidden shared-record concept is no longer minted into fresh versions by default

generated legacy schema ids are less location-bound too: new create, update, and seed flows now use stable form-based ids like form.urine instead of encoding the old folder or group name into the schema id

the schema seed/reset path is more tree-first too: ensure_reference_seed now creates real container and form nodes first, then backfills legacy location mirrors from those nodes instead of seeding forms through the old grouped-first path

ensure_library_tree is more tree-first too: it no longer orchestrates sync work in old group_order and form_order order, it prefers real node parent/order first, and it now uses the shared form-node and container helpers for rebuilds instead of manually recreating grouped-first state

direct model-layer placeholder writes are more isolated too: create and seed flows now build FormDefinition rows through one small compatibility-shell helper instead of open-coding group_* values in multiple places
stale FormDefinition.common_field_set_id values no longer survive active tree sync either: the same compatibility-sync path now clears that old mirror field back to None
the FormDefinition model contract is less legacy too: old group_name, group_kind, group_order, and form_order columns are now nullable compatibility shadows instead of required first-class fields
runtime schema migration now upgrades older SQLite DBs to that nullable shape too, so existing local DBs no longer force placeholder writes just to satisfy the old grouped model
the reset script now follows that same runtime migration path too, so a fresh sample DB no longer regresses back to the old NOT NULL grouped-column shape

the last-resort tree rebuild fallback is safer too: if a definition loses its node, parent key, and all usable legacy location hints, it now rebuilds as a top-level form instead of inventing a self-named folder from missing legacy data

stored version metadata is cleaner too: startup and reset backfill now strip stale common_field_set_id from old schema_json and block_schema_json, and they normalize old legacy_form_* block meta into cleaner form_* keys with stable form.<slug> ids

the save payload alias path is thinner too: group_name is now just a tolerated legacy input alias that gets normalized into location_name and removed before validation, instead of lingering as part of the active request shape

top-level compatibility shadows are more honest too: when a form lives at the root, legacy group_name, group_kind, and group_order are now kept NULL instead of being filled with a fake self-named group
grouped compatibility shadows are thinner too: live tree sync no longer actively maintains legacy group_kind; grouped fallback now relies on group_name and ordering hints only
stale self-named top-level shadows are safer too: if an old form still carries a fake self-named group_name, rebuild fallback now treats that as top-level instead of recreating a bogus folder
the stored model shape is thinner too: FormDefinition.group_kind is gone from the live SQLAlchemy and runtime DB shape, and older local DBs are rebuilt forward automatically on startup

the stored model shape is thinner again too: FormDefinition.common_field_set_id is gone from the live SQLAlchemy and runtime DB shape now too, and older local DBs are rebuilt forward automatically on startup

the stored model shape is thinner again too: FormDefinition.group_order is gone from the live SQLAlchemy and runtime DB shape, and grouped fallback now preserves existing container order instead of mirroring that old field

the stored model shape is thinner again too: FormDefinition.group_name is gone from the live SQLAlchemy and runtime DB shape, and if a form loses both its real node and parent key, fallback now treats it as top-level instead of trusting old grouped-name shadows

the stored model shape is thinner again too: FormDefinition.form_order is gone from the live SQLAlchemy and runtime DB shape, and rebuild fallback now uses the current version schema order instead of a separate legacy mirror column

the backend resolver path is thinner too: ensure_library_tree now reads schema order directly instead of going through a leftover legacy location-hint wrapper, and resolve_form_location_metadata no longer returns unused resolved_parent_name or resolved_parent_order scaffolding

the live contract is thinner too: save validation no longer maps group_name into location_name, and /builder new-draft startup now reads only location_name instead of tolerating old grouped-era query params
the live save contract is thinner too: the builder now posts form_schema directly, and backend validation no longer remaps old schema into form_schema

the live save contract is stricter too: FormSavePayload now expects the block-based form_schema shape in active API usage, so old fields and sections save payloads are no longer part of the live builder contract
the live form-read contract is thinner too: /api/forms/{slug} now returns only block_schema for the active form shape, and the builder derives any temporary legacy projection locally

backend naming is more honest too: the no-op save alias validator is gone, and the remaining form-definition helpers in services.py now read like tree-first helpers instead of grouped-era compatibility names
the active create/update path is thinner too: block-based save payloads now normalize directly through a block-first storage helper instead of passing through older schema-bridge wrappers

the active create/update entry path is thinner too: it no longer materializes a full legacy schema just to derive slug/name before saving; live saves now read those directly from payload and block metadata
active block metadata is cleaner too: new writes now use form_id, form_key, and form_order in block_schema.meta, while old legacy_form_* keys are only read and backfilled for stored compatibility
active block source metadata is more honest too: live builder-created block schemas now store source_kind = builder_blocks_v1, while true legacy conversions still keep the compatibility marker
the live backend block-meta path is thinner too: active create/update conversion no longer reads legacy_form_* keys, and those old keys are now only part of startup cleanup/backfill logic

version storage assembly is more honest too: create, update, and seed now go through explicit storage builders and one FormVersion storage-record helper, so schema_json reads more clearly as compatibility storage instead of a parallel live model

live read and startup cleanup are more explicit too: serialize_form and startup backfill now go through dedicated storage-document loaders, and the startup pass is named around full form-version storage cleanup instead of only block_schema backfill
old legacy_form_* block meta is thinner still: startup cleanup now drops those keys without reading them as fallback inputs, and the frontend draft meta sync strips legacy_form_id too

live frontend node helpers are thinner too: core builder helpers now treat blocks as the only active node shape, instead of falling back to old fields and sections style field objects in the live UI path
live option data is more consistent too: the builder now uses option name as the active key end to end, and old label values are only normalized away during helper cleanup
focused section and field cards are thinner too: they now render directly from the passed live block node instead of re-looking up the same path and carrying fallback source juggling

mixed-content render helpers are more aligned too: the live builder now uses item-oriented helper naming for shared section, group, and field render paths instead of pretending those helpers are field-only
active builder selection state is more aligned too: the shared item selection path and item focus and toggle actions now use item naming instead of field naming in the live JS flow

internal preview and traversal helpers are more aligned too: shared item-path, item-summary, preview-item, and item-count helpers now read like mixed-content block code instead of field-era helper code

mixed-content organizer chrome is more aligned too: the live builder now uses item-* organizer, list, and focus CSS and markup naming instead of field-* naming for shared section, group, and utility content flows

shared item-card chrome is more aligned too: the live builder now uses item-* card, head, meta, summary, title, focus, basics, and input CSS and markup naming instead of field-* naming for shared mixed-content cards
preview segment plumbing is more aligned too: the live preview now groups shared root content under item segment naming instead of carrying fields as the internal mixed-content segment shape
live choice seeding is cleaner too: when an input switches to Choices, the first seeded option now uses the active name shape directly instead of minting the old label key
blank item factories are more honest too: field and group creation now use separate helpers, and shared insertion helpers no longer pretend every mixed content add path is field-only

input-type internals are more aligned too: the live builder now uses input-oriented helper naming for control, data, unit, normal, options, and type logic instead of keeping those paths under broader field helper names
active input props are leaner too: the live builder now relies on control plus data_type instead of minting a duplicate field_type mirror in new drafts and edits
stored block cleanup is leaner too: startup and save cleanup now strips stale field_type and old option label residue from active block-schema storage
legacy-to-block option conversion is cleaner too: when older storage is bridged into block schema, options now come out with the active name shape directly instead of minting old label keys first

form-version storage assembly is cleaner too: raw live block payloads are normalized before the legacy storage bridge runs, so the live save path no longer depends on fallback reads of stale option label keys
old option label residue is thinner in the browser too: legacy label to name cleanup now lives in draft-ingress normalization, while repeated live option helpers read the active name shape only

seed and missing-block fallback storage are cleaner too: both now rebuild block storage through one explicit legacy-storage to block-storage bridge helper instead of open-coding direct legacy block conversion paths

form-choice payloads are more location-first too: builder quick-switch reads now prefer location_path_label and explicit form_path_label, instead of depending on older path_label or location_label aliases in the live browser path

live form-choice payloads are thinner too: list_form_choices no longer emits the old path_label or location_label aliases, and active builder, new, and move flows now read the explicit location-first keys directly

live container-choice payloads are thinner too: list_container_choices now exposes explicit folder_path_label, and builder, new, and move folder flows no longer depend on the old generic path_label alias

browser-side path naming is more explicit too: duplicate form choices now use data-form-path-label, and the remaining location helpers distinguish form_path_label from folder_path_label instead of carrying a generic pathLabel pocket
form create, update, and move flows now use a shared tree-first form-node sync helper, so the real LibraryNode state is updated directly before legacy mirrors are backfilled
resolve_form_location_metadata is more tree-first too: it now feeds create/update with resolved_parent_* and resolved_form_order values instead of returning group_* as the primary active shape

legacy group_* mirror backfill is now centralized too: one helper derives those compatibility fields from the real node state instead of duplicating that logic across create/update/move/tree-sync paths
top-level new and copied drafts are cleaner too: they no longer start from the old Unassigned sentinel or keep a stale previous form name as fake location state
forms/new root-mode handoff is more explicit too: it now passes location_name = Top level into /builder instead of using the old form-name placeholder for top-level drafts

the active browser path is stricter too: the builder no longer treats location_name equal to the form name as a top-level shortcut, so top-level handling in the live UI now depends on explicit Top level state instead of that old placeholder convention

the live save resolver matches that now too: backend create and update no longer treat location_name equal to the form name as top-level, so same-name locations are resolved as real folders unless Top level is sent explicitly
old Unassigned location residue is thinner too: stale inputs are now normalized once at ingress to Top level, instead of being special-cased across multiple live location helpers
the builder frontend reads more tree-first too: active suggestion helpers and setup variables now use location language instead of old group wording where the UI already treats folders as locations

the active create/update resolver is more tree-first too: it now derives parent/order state from real library nodes and the current location intent, instead of depending on old group_kind/group_order/form_order inputs
the /forms/new flow reads more consistently now too: its template and browser-side logic use location wording for the visible create flow, and the handoff into /builder now uses location_name only

serialized form payloads are more tree-first too: serialize_form now returns location_name, location_path_label, location_node_key, and location_kind, and the builder display/helpers use those aliases directly
the active save API is more tree-first too: the current builder now posts location_name, and the live save contract no longer treats group_name as an active alias

active read compatibility is more tree-first too: legacy group_* metadata is now derived from the real library tree during serialization and grouped listings, instead of trusting the raw stored legacy columns
the visible builder setup flow is more tree-first too: the Location input now binds to location_name instead of the old group_name, while the draft keeps its real location state synchronized directly
the active builder draft is more tree-first too: location_* is now the only active location state in the editor

the active builder setup internals are thinner too: Key and Notes now bind straight to block_schema.meta, and the live draft no longer keeps a duplicated draft.schema object just to drive those fields

the active organizer and render path is thinner too: content organizers, focused cards, and preview section labels now read directly from block nodes instead of carrying a synthetic legacy .view projection through the live builder UI
the live frontend draft contract is thinner too: the builder no longer accepts a form_schema fallback when hydrating local drafts, and now expects block_schema directly in the active UI path
the live frontend block state is more honest too: new drafts and edited drafts now keep source_kind = builder_blocks_v1 instead of reusing the old compatibility label
the save payload schema is leaner too: group_kind, group_order, and form_order are no longer part of FormSavePayload, and old callers can still send them as ignored compatibility extras
dead grouped-library backend helpers are gone too: the codebase no longer keeps list_grouped_forms / split_library_groups around even though the live app is already tree-first
the old separate Arrange pane is gone; Advanced now means deeper controls inside the same Content editor
builder action wording is quieter too: Duplicate now reads Copy, More is shorter and calmer than the old More options, and destructive actions now prefer Remove language for a less tool-like feel
toggle and preview wording are calmer too: setup, save, and section cards now use Show and Hide instead of Open and Done, and preview helper copy now says Choose or Show instead of Open
selected sections still use a compact section organizer plus one focused section editor
selected sections and groups use compact item organizers plus one focused item editor
dropdown fields now use compact choice organizers plus one focused choice editor
form details and save now use narrower calmer guided surfaces
folder input now suggests existing library folders
save now shows a clearer draft-state spotlight with a simpler optional note field
the top shell now uses a lighter status bar and a more compact workspace header

the builder chrome is lighter now too: the old feedback bar is gone, draft status now lives in the top app bar with the main actions, and the technical JSON panel starts hidden until advanced-mode work actually needs it
the preview is now read-only and includes sticky quick-jump section navigation
advanced mode now keeps actual top-level ordered-block editing inside the same Content pane
the preview now respects root block order instead of forcing one single ungrouped-fields area before all sections
advanced Content editing now exposes real note, divider, and table blocks while the calm default panes still hide them
selected sections can now add note and divider blocks in advanced mode, while the default section editor hides those utility blocks behind a quiet hint
the left outline and library wording now use calmer non-technical labels like basics, content, location, and edit
the builder basics flow is clearer too: it now says Name and Location, and top-level forms read as Top level instead of the old Unassigned
item editors are calmer too: fields, options, and utility items now use Name instead of the more technical Label
the builder Location field is now tree-aware too: it suggests real folder paths from the persisted container tree and resolves the correct parent container when one is selected
the builder quick-switch drawer is tree-aware now too: it searches and lists forms by real folder path instead of the old one-level grouped drawer
the builder bootstrap path is tree-first now too: quick-switch data comes from form_choices, not the old grouped builder payload
the builder bootstrap payload no longer sends old shared-metadata options either, since that control is no longer part of the visible builder flow
the current builder save path is less legacy too: it no longer posts old grouping/order fields, and the backend now derives folder/order metadata from the real tree when possible
the committed sample runtime DB has been reset to clean schema-seeded state and can be reset again with tools/scripts/reset_builder_runtime_db.py
the focused field editor is now lighter, with drag left in the organizer and a more compact basics and choices treatment
the focused section editor is now lighter too, with drag left in the organizer and a more compact section basics treatment
selected section and field destructive actions now live in a quieter footer more-options area
the sections pane is now less mechanical, with a simple count, no organizer row numbers, and an editing badge only on the active row
current_implementation_checkpoint.current_builder_surface_note: The builder is now block-backed and tree-first in the live path. The visible workspace uses one real Content editor for root and nested editing, and Advanced now means deeper controls on that same pane instead of a separate Arrange or Layout lane.

phase_1_goals: Define options, normal values, and unit hints
current_product_lock: the client is a small clinic, so the app should feel premium through calmness, clarity, speed, and reliability instead of enterprise workflow complexity
the builder is now a setup/admin tool, not the main daily surface of the product
the main daily user should be the medtech
most day to day use should happen in records or data-entry runtime, not in the builder
do not default to lab admin, pathologist, reviewer, or approval-chain workflow unless the real clinic process explicitly requires it

core_decisions: print output is patient-facing and should be compact
target one page when content allows it
use A4 portrait rather than copying the old landscape templates
header/accent color is configurable per form
patient info is not a hardcoded engine zone; users create and configure those fields in the form builder
medtech/pathologist are generic configurable signatory slots, not ordinary result fields and not hardcoded employee concepts
required fields are ordinary field-level builder props
print config belongs in the form builder under a separate Print pane/tab
use constrained template controls rather than a freeform Canva-style editor
implemented: field-level required toggle in the builder
completion validation for builder-marked required fields
generic record_identity config at block_schema.meta.record_identity
current forms now carry a normal editable Patient Information builder group from the original default_lab_request fields, with Name and Case Number required and wired into record identity/search
print_config at block_schema.meta.print_config
signatory config at block_schema.meta.signatories
builder Print pane for accent color, density, show/hide controls, configurable summary rows, controlled result-body options, and result layout
builder Signatories pane for role labels, dropdown people, fixed people, fixed stamp images, manual entry, blank lines, required completion, print visibility, signature lines, and license display
fixed stamp image signatories are generic and can be used when a role has a full uploaded signature/name/license image; this is not hardcoded to Pathologist

Medical Technologist and Pathologist were removed from normal Patient Information fields and migrated into generic signatories; current seeded defaults are two medtech slots and one fixed pathologist slot
backend print_config normalization plus print summary and generic signatory resolution
builder-side print preview generated from the unsaved draft with sample data and an estimated one-page fit signal
shared print-page macro used by builder preview and /records/{id}/print
controlled result-body print options for hide empty fields, section headings, group headings, image size, and table density
footer/signatory configuration is now generic rather than left/right source-specific
compact result-grid layout compresses consecutive ordinary scalar fields into a two-column print grid while keeping image fields full width
first automated real-form fit audit after compact grid reduced the seeded sample set to 0 long estimates
Chromium PDF QA confirmed the remaining tight forms OGTT, Semen, and Serology each export as one A4 page after the generic print spacing pass

clinic-like stress PDF QA confirmed OGTT, Semen, and Serology still export as one A4 page with longer patient names, case numbers, requesting physician, medtech/pathologist names, remarks, and release fields
actual /records/{id}/print smoke now passes across all current 16 forms using temporary completed records; the QA script snapshots/restores the runtime DB by default

browser PDF QA now exists at tools/scripts/print_pdf_qa.py and checks real Playwright-generated A4 PDF page counts while restoring the runtime DB by default; rerun it on Windows for the current 16 forms because WSL currently lacks Playwright Chromium Linux system libraries
real record print pages now receive document.fit_estimate and show the estimated fit badge in the toolbar
/records/{id}/print applies print_config
existing Semen sample verified as one A4 portrait page
known_limits: one-page output cannot be guaranteed for arbitrarily long forms
existing records use their frozen form_version unless migrated intentionally
summary rows support field/system sources but no advanced conditional expressions yet
footer/signatory layout is intentionally constrained to a compact auto-fit signature footer
real production records and the clinic's actual browser/printer settings still need final real-device print QA
current output is browser-print based, not a dedicated server-side PDF engine

next_major_build_scope: test print output with real clinic records and real printer/browser settings
test installed desktop app behavior on a clean Windows PC
test same-network LAN access from another trusted clinic device using hostname, IP fallback, and QR
finish clean-PC restore drill and Windows validation of scheduled/external/pre-update backup behavior
consider print design overrides only if real print review proves a concrete need
treat non-print UI work as secondary cleanup unless direct issues appear

important_rules: do not hardcode shared_patient_info as a mandatory builder zone
do not hardcode medtech or pathologist as special engine concepts; model them as configurable signatory slots
hardcode primitives instead of medical assumptions
keep the engine highly flexible while keeping the UI simple by default
supporting_concepts: optional reusable blocks later if they can return without harming simplicity
search and tags later in addition to hierarchy

main_rules: one main editing context visible at a time
do not expose schema language in the primary UI
keep live preview reassuring, not dominant

important_domain_rules: COVID 19 Antigen (Rapid Test) belongs under Serology
Shared patient/request metadata still exists as a backend compatibility field set, but it is intentionally hidden from the visible builder flow
Blood Bank > Type of Crossmatching > VITAL SIGNS is a field_group
Blood Chemistry > Male and Female require IONIZED CALCIUM immediately after CHLORIDE
known_conservative_areas: Some Blood Bank phase result fields
implementation_warning: Do not hardcode one UI screen per exam
Do not use legacy print templates as the primary data model
Do not make the client dependent on programmers for ordinary exam changes

builder_v2_direction.summary: Reduce mental load first. The builder should feel calm, step-like, and non-technical.

implemented_improvements: recursive in-place content canvas for root and nested containers and fields
fresh recursive IDs for copied block subtrees and copied choice options
hidden-advanced-safe SortableJS collection mapping
advanced utility-card drag handles
compact responsive workspace tabs above the editor on tablet and mobile
real-browser duplicate, nested-edit, copy/remove, reorder, save/reload, light/dark, desktop, tablet, and mobile QA
collapsible form library drawer
docked side-by-side live preview panel on desktop
preview promoted to a first-class live-preview callout in the main flow
single main builder canvas
left outline navigation for form details, free fields, sections, and save
collapsed-by-default setup card for existing forms
collapsible top-of-form area
collapsible sections
one-section-at-a-time focus editing
compact section organizer with one focused section editor
compact item organizers with one focused item editor in free fields and selected sections
compact choice organizers with one focused choice editor in dropdown fields
narrower guided form-details and save surfaces
folder suggestions from existing library groups
clearer draft-state spotlight in the save surface
lighter first-glance top shell
sticky quick-jump section navigation inside preview
one-field-at-a-time edit flow
calmer open/done plus more action menus
separate save step card
drag-and-drop section ordering
drag-and-drop field ordering
local SortableJS-powered ordering layer
floating save/reset dock while dirty
unsaved-change protection on form switch and page unload
clean-state save button that disables itself and shows Saved
save step collapsed by default until needed
compact closed section and field rows with reduced helper-text repetition
live shell summary updates while editing the form title
preview control moved out of the crowded top bar and into a dedicated live-preview callout
preview stays visible while editing instead of blocking the builder in an overlay
preview panel updates live while editing builder fields and form titles
desktop preview panel uses its own internal scroll area for long forms
preview hide/show is hardened so a hidden preview is removed from layout instead of leaking below the editor
persistent helper copy reduced into small question-mark popovers in key cards
open help popovers and action menus close when the user clicks elsewhere
in-app destructive and unsaved-change decisions now use a calmer custom modal instead of browser confirm dialogs
floating dirty-state save dock is now smaller and less visually aggressive
top bar actions are now shorter and more compact
drag and more controls are now icon-like and visually quieter
drag and more controls stay subdued until a row is active or hovered
open section cards use a compact builder-head row where title and quick add-actions stay together
save step uses a compact inline row with note input plus one save action
open field cards use lighter metadata chrome with less repetitive label noise
stage header includes an advanced-mode toggle that defaults to off
advanced blocks and technical json are hidden while advanced mode is off
nested choice organizers are less mechanical: no choice row numbers, only the active choice shows Editing, and the selected choice uses a calmer spotlight card
selected choice actions now use a quieter footer More options area with Duplicate and a calmer confirmed Delete flow
the left outline is lighter: section subitems no longer show per-row counts, and only the active section shows Editing
the top shell is calmer: the status strip is subtler, status messages are shorter, and the live-preview callout uses steadier less repetitive copy
the top-right app-bar actions are quieter: New and Save stay visible, while Duplicate now lives in a small overflow menu
the preview panel header is quieter: shorter heading/copy, shorter hide action, and less repeated live/read-only wording inside the preview card
the left rail header is quieter: This form became Outline, the extra summary line was removed, and the navigation block reads less like a mini dashboard
the center workspace header is quieter: Editing became Builder, the title now uses the form name directly, and the supporting copy is shorter and less repetitive
the focused section, field, group, and choice strips are quieter: they now use small label-style cues instead of sentence-style Editing this copy
the top-level outline is quieter too: Ungrouped fields and Sections no longer show count chips, so the left rail reads more like navigation than a mini manager
the center and preview chrome are quieter too: the builder header, Sections header, and preview surfaces no longer repeat summary count badges

the center organizer metadata is quieter too: section rows no longer repeat item counts, field rows use simpler type cues like Dropdown, and the choices editor no longer repeats choice counts in its header and spotlight
the preview wording is calmer too: shorter Show and Hide actions, Live preview callout language, and preview copy that reads more like a live companion than a technical sample panel
the empty-state and no-data copy are warmer too: builder and library messages now guide the user more gently instead of sounding like raw system states

the save surface is calmer too: Save draft is now just Save, the finish-step wording is softer, the floating save dock is quieter, and the save card now stays in sync with dirty state while editing the note
the library page is calmer too: folder jump counts and repeated folder metadata are gone, and form card actions now use shorter labels like Copy and Edit
the library cards are lighter too: version labels are subtler (v1), card spacing is tighter, and the library now reads more like a calm folder browser than an admin list
the top of the library page is calmer too: shorter header copy, quieter Find search affordance, and a lighter New action for a cleaner first glance
the library header itself is tighter too: spacing is calmer, the top band sits lower visually, and the first impression is less crowded
remaining_builder_v2_gaps: confirmed bug fixes from actual use
real clinic-device and real-data print QA before optional builder expansion

primary_ui_rules: Hide internal keys and ordering metadata from the main flow
Use direct manipulation for ordering instead of typed order values
Do not keep library, builder, and preview equally visible at all times
recommended_frontend_approach: Current code: server-rendered HTML + vanilla JS + SortableJS
Alpine.js is optional future helper direction, not current repo reality
implementation_phases: Phase 2: rebuild the core editing flow

ideal_phase_1_outcome: The client can create or edit an exam/form without developer help
The resulting schema stays structured and versionable
The app is ready for later input and print flows
